channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran picks new leader. And, Trump won't sign bills until Congress overhauls voting
item.description: Iran has named Mojtaba Khamenei as its new supreme leader. And, President Trump says he will not sign any more bills until Congress overhauls voting.
item.content:encoded:
Iran has named Mojtaba Khamenei as its new supreme leader. And, President Trump says he will not sign any more bills until Congress overhauls voting.

(Image credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Chimps' taste for fermented fruit hints at origins of human love of alcohol
item.description: Scientists analyzed the urine of wild chimpanzees who'd feasted on fallen fruit to see how much alcohol they consumed from the fermented sugars.
item.content:encoded:
Scientists analyzed the urine of wild chimpanzees who'd feasted on fallen fruit to see how much alcohol they consumed from the fermented sugars.

(Image credit: Sharifah Namaganda)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Iran attacks Israel and Gulf states after naming a new supreme leader on Day 10 of war
item.description: The price of crude oil briefly neared $120 a barrel Monday as Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei the supreme leader and then launched new attacks at Israel and Gulf states.
item.content:encoded:
The price of crude oil briefly neared $120 a barrel Monday as Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei the supreme leader and then launched new attacks at Israel and Gulf states.

(Image credit: Bilal Hussein)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: World shares tumble as Iran war pushes crude prices over $110 a barrel
item.description: World shares tumbled on Monday, with Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 index plunging more than 5%, after oil prices spiked at nearly $120 a barrel.
item.content:encoded:
World shares tumbled on Monday, with Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 index plunging more than 5%, after oil prices spiked at nearly $120 a barrel.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Come along with some geese as they migrate back from their southern winter havens
item.description: Geese's iconic "V" formations and trademark squawks can be seen and heard overhead as they go back and forth to the south through the year. But what does it take for such a long trip?
item.content:encoded:
Geese's iconic "V" formations and trademark squawks can be seen and heard overhead as they go back and forth to the south through the year. But what does it take for such a long trip?

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: The next redistricting battle might be who is counted in state legislative districts
item.description: A Republican push to alter the census may lead to a radical shift in redistricting for state legislatures — drawing districts that don't take into account children and non-U.S. citizen adults.
item.content:encoded:
A Republican push to alter the census may lead to a radical shift in redistricting for state legislatures — drawing districts that don't take into account children and non-U.S. citizen adults.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Millions more people are in the path of rising seas than previously thought
item.description: Oceans are rising as the climate changes, threatening coastal cities. A new study shows that much more of the world's population is vulnerable than earlier predictions had estimated.
item.content:encoded:
Oceans are rising as the climate changes, threatening coastal cities. A new study shows that much more of the world's population is vulnerable than earlier predictions had estimated.

(Image credit: Josh Edelson)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sommer

item.title: Kids' willpower is no match for fast food and screens. Try this instead
item.description: For decades, parents were told to help children build willpower like a muscle, to resist things like junk food and too much time on their screens. But new research suggests a better strategy.
item.content:encoded: For decades, parents were told to help children build willpower like a muscle, to resist things like junk food and too much time on their screens. But new research suggests a better strategy.
item.dc:creator: Michaeleen Doucleff

item.title: Israel needs weeks to destroy Iran's military, defense official says
item.description: A senior Israeli defense official tells NPR that Israel needs three more weeks to accomplish its goal of decimating Iran's military forces.
item.content:encoded: A senior Israeli defense official tells NPR that Israel needs three more weeks to accomplish its goal of decimating Iran's military forces.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: 12 years on, renewed hunt for missing Malaysia Airlines flight comes up empty
item.description: Twelve years after Malaysia Airlines Flight MH370 vanished with 239 people aboard, a deep-sea search has so far failed to locate the missing aircraft, as families pressed for the effort to continue.
item.content:encoded:
Twelve years after Malaysia Airlines Flight MH370 vanished with 239 people aboard, a deep-sea search has so far failed to locate the missing aircraft, as families pressed for the effort to continue.

(Image credit: Vincent Thian)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
